title: Color Schemes
text: discrete palettes, ask fewer colors full range, get first colors palette. palettes works fine. example, Dark2 palette RColorBrewer: use palette color iris PCA: three colors lefthand side swatch used color species. However, discrete palettes ordering , e.g. go red blue via yellow. ’s rainbow jcolors package: PCA embedding now looks like: prefer use fuller extent palette, can treat palette continuous, appending ::c name color scheme, e.g. "jcolors::rainbow::c". Now result : colors come left-, right-center positions swatch. downside treating palettes continuous guarantee interpolation result colors actually come palette. fact, probably won’t. just got lucky example, interpolating colors required. colors show natural progression like jcolors::rainbow, results still ok. However, palettes like RColorBrewer::Dark2, interpolation may turn well. iris PCA “continuous” version Dark2, .e. specifying RColorBrewer::Dark2::c results : left cluster uses green left-hand side Dark2 swatch, right cluster colored gray color right-hand side. middle cluster isn’t colors mixes rather murkily gray cluster. doesn’t make sense use interpolation case. summary, avoid interpolation discrete color schemes can, definitely avoid like RColorBrewer::Dark2 don’t work color scale.

title: Getting Started
text: Create plot first two principal components (PCA) iris dataset: Simplest use embed_plot: pass data frame use last (case, ) factor column finds stable built-Polychrome 36 categorical palette. Numeric vectors instead use sequential HCL Viridis palette default. built-categorical palette available, Vizier uses HCL Dynamic fallback. explicitly color iris species, use rainbow color scheme also provide title subtitle: Increase transparency fill color scaling alpha 0.5: already colors want use point, can use colors parameter. example , colorRampPalette(c("red", "yellow"))(nrow(iris)) produces vector 150 colors going red yellow: just want points one color need pass single value, e.g.  colors = "blue". general, pass fewer colors points, colors recycled. ’s another example using built-palette, topo.colors: package also includes turbo colormap palette, via turbo function, works lot like grDevices::rainbow (although reversed terms colors): rev argument reverses generated color scheme mapped categories numeric values. reorder explicitly supplied per-row colors: can also provide palette (.e. vector colors): Note colors palette needed, extra ones ignored: e.g. c("black", "red", "gray", "blue"), "blue" unused, needed three colors palette plot three species. fully named palette maps categories name, remains stable reordering subsetting. Extra names ignored; every observed category must named color: Watch opposite situation need colors palette provides. case vizier use interpolation get colors needs. might work palettes represent continuous color scale (like rainbow), give weird probably undesirable results discrete palettes. details, see Discrete Palettes continuous Type section color schemes article. R 4.0, new color palettes. can see options available via grDevices::palette.pals() generate palette using grDevices::palette.colors. ’s example using "Okabe-Ito" palette: palette palette.pals, can also just provide palette name shortcut: use common limits equal physical X/Y units, preventing clusters stretched non-square device: can plot category names instead points, looks bad ’re long (dataset large). Making text bit smaller cex param can help: color schemes, Vizier makes use excellent paletteer package. can select one palettes offer (among ways) passing string format "package::palette". example, use Dark2 scheme RColorBrewer package (based ColorBrewer schemes): selecting color schemes, see Color schemes article. ’s another example, using continuous palette RColorBrewer, useful mapping numeric vectors color: top selects exactly many finite numeric values decreasing order; ties resolved existing row order. Direct character vectors treated categories unless every non-missing entry literal color. Character columns discovered data frame remain conservatively inferred factor-like, avoiding accidental coloring identifier columns.
code: pca_iris <- stats::prcomp(iris[, -5], retx = TRUE, rank. = 2) embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris) embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris$Species, color_scheme = rainbow, title = "iris PCA", sub = "rainbow color scheme") embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris$Species, color_scheme = rainbow, alpha_scale = 0.5) my_iris_colors = colorRampPalette(c("red", "yellow"))(nrow(iris)) embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris$Species, colors = my_iris_colors) embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris$Species, color_scheme = turbo) embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris$Species, color_scheme = turbo, rev = TRUE) embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris$Species, color_scheme = c("black", "red", "gray")) species_colors <- c(setosa = "#E69F00", versicolor = "#56B4E9", virginica = "#009E73") embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris$Species, color_scheme = species_colors) if (exists("palette.colors", where = "package:grDevices")) {   embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris$Species, color_scheme = palette.colors(palette = "Okabe-Ito")) } embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris$Species, color_scheme = "Okabe-Ito") embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris$Species, color_scheme = topo.colors, equal_axes = TRUE) embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris$Species, cex = 0.75, text = iris$Species) embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris, color_scheme = "RColorBrewer::Dark2") # Visualize numeric value (petal length) as a color embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris$Petal.Length, color_scheme = "RColorBrewer::Blues") # Just show the points with the 10 longest petals embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris$Petal.Length, color_scheme = "RColorBrewer::Blues", top = 10)

title: Getting Started
text: install ggplot2 package, embed_ggplot() returns ordinary ggplot object coordinate color handling embed_plot(). makes easy add ggplot2 layers, labels, themes. example, can add ellipse species: Numeric inputs use native continuous ggplot2 scale colorbar. usual ggplot2 labeling theme functions can added Vizier continues handle colors fixed coordinates:
code: iris_ggplot <- embed_ggplot(   pca_iris$x,   iris$Species,   cex = 2,   title = "iris PCA" )  iris_ggplot +   ggplot2::stat_ellipse(level = 0.8, linewidth = 0.8) +   ggplot2::labs(color = "Species", subtitle = "80% confidence ellipses") +   ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 12) embed_ggplot(   pca_iris$x,   iris$Petal.Length,   cex = 2,   equal_axes = TRUE,   title = "iris petal length" ) +   ggplot2::labs(color = "Petal length") +   ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

title: Getting Started
text: install plotly package, can use embed_plotly function interface embed_plot (except top sub parameters missing). advantage showing categorical legends numeric colorbars tooltips. text works numeric colors well; default hover text preserves original row numbers without showing literal input colors:
code: embed_plotly(pca_iris$x, iris, color_scheme = rainbow) # Don't have to see a legend if custom tooltips will do embed_plotly(   pca_iris$x,   iris,   color_scheme = rainbow,   show_legend = FALSE,   tooltip = paste("Species:", iris$Species) )

title: Visualization of 2D Datasets in R
text: R Package Visualization 2D Datasets. Visualizing datasets 2D (e.g. via PCA, Sammon Mapping, t-SNE) much informative points colored, using something like: Factor levels mapped different colors. numeric value mapped color scale. string encoding color. package make bit easier, using graphics::plot function, returned ggplot2 object, plotly JavaScript library. don’t specify specific column color , attempt find suitable factor color column automatically, using last suitable column found, can add custom column dataframe needed picked automatically.

title: Embedding Plot Using ggplot2 — embed_ggplot
text: Plots embedded coordinates ggplot2 returns ordinary ggplot object, callers can add themes, annotations, guides, ggplot layers.

title: Embedding Plot Using ggplot2 — embed_ggplot
code: embed_ggplot(   coords,   x = NULL,   colors = NULL,   color_scheme = NULL,   num_colors = 15,   alpha_scale = 1,   limits = NULL,   top = NULL,   cex = 1,   title = NULL,   text = NULL,   sub = NULL,   equal_axes = FALSE,   pc_axes = FALSE,   xlim = NULL,   ylim = NULL,   show_axes = TRUE,   NA_color = NULL,   rev = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Embedding Plot Using ggplot2 — embed_ggplot
text: coords Matrix embedded coordinates, many rows observations, 2 columns. x Either data frame column can used derive suitable vector colors. Ignored colors provided. colors single color one color per observation. color_scheme color scheme. See Details. Ignored colors specified. num_colors Number distinct colors use palette, x numeric vector, assumption palette continuous (probably ). Ignored x numeric vector. set NULL, set length(x). alpha_scale Scale opacity alpha colors, 0 1. Useful increasing transparency points, especially large plots lots overlap. limits range colors map mapping numeric vector. specified, range x. useful external absolute scale used. Ignored x numeric vector. top NULL, many finite values directly supplied numeric x displayed. Values selected decreasing order existing row order breaking ties. cex Size passed ggplot2 point text layer. title Title plot. text Vector label text display instead point. labels long data set large, unlikely legible, occasionally useful. sub Subtitle plot. Appears title. equal_axes TRUE, use common coordinate range equal physical units X Y axes. pc_axes TRUE, coords replaced first two (unscaled) principal components, effect rotating data (potential reflection) main variance aligns along X-axis. scaling effect. xlim Vector two numeric values give numeric extent X-axis PCA rotation. Ignored equal_axes = TRUE. ylim Vector two numeric values give numeric extent Y-axis PCA rotation. Ignored equal_axes = TRUE. show_axes TRUE, axes, axis labels (frame) displayed. NA_color Color use NA values, can arise using factor column x (item colors NA). default, points displayed. rev Logical indicating whether generated palettes continuous scales reversed. Explicit colors unchanged. verbose TRUE, log messages console, mainly searching suitable color column dataframe.

title: Embedding Plot — embed_plot
text: x argument can used provide suitable vector colors either data frame vector. data frame provided, vector colors looked . present, used colors argument directly. Otherwise, factor column looked , level mapped different color. Otherwise, one color used point. one column type found data frame, last one encountered used. vector provided, similar procedure data frame used mapping content vector colors. Additionally, numeric vector can provided, linearly mapped color scheme. color_scheme parameter can one : palette function takes integer n returns vector colors, e.g. grDevices::rainbow(). applicable functions, see Palettes help page grDevices package (e.g. running ?rainbow command). vector colors making custom palette devising, e.g. c("red", "green", "blue"). must least two colors list. name color scheme provided paletteer package, form "package::palette". examples include "dutchmasters::milkmaid", "cartography::green.pal", "viridis::inferno" "RColorBrewer::Dark2". colors required supported color scheme, interpolation used create required number colors. name pre-defined R palette, running R 4.0 later. examples include "Okabe-Ito", "Tableau 10" "Alphabet". See grDevices::palette.pals() list possible names. Note function available prior R 4.0. details, see https://developer.r-project.org/Blog/public/2019/11/21/-new-palette--r/index.html. Numeric vectors use sequential HCL Viridis palette default. Factor directly supplied character vectors use built-"Polychrome 36" palette stable order available, HCL Dynamic palette otherwise. x omitted, categorical palette rule assigns one color per row. Data-frame character columns remain eligible conservatively factor-like, identifier columns inferred categories. fully named color_scheme vector maps observed category values name; extra entries ignored missing entries error. rev = TRUE reverses generated palettes continuous scales mapping rows, reorder explicit per-observation colors. just want one color points, can pass single color colors argument, e.g. colors = "blue".

title: Embedding Plot — embed_plot
code: # Embed with PCA pca_iris <- stats::prcomp(iris[, -5], retx = TRUE, rank. = 2) # Visualize the resulting embedding, colored by iris species, using the # rainbow color scheme embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris$Species,   color_scheme = rainbow,   title = "iris PCA", sub = "rainbow colors" )   # topo.colors scheme embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris$Species, color_scheme = topo.colors)   # Pass in data frame and it will use the last (in this case, only) factor # column it finds embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris)   # Use the "Dark2" RColorBrewer scheme embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris, color_scheme = "RColorBrewer::Dark2")   # Can plot the category names instead of points, but looks bad if they're # long (or the dataset is large) embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris$Species, cex = 0.5, text = iris$Species)   # Visualize numeric value (petal length) as a color embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris$Petal.Length, color_scheme = "RColorBrewer::Blues")   # Just show the points with the 10 longest petals embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris$Petal.Length, color_scheme = "RColorBrewer::Blues", top = 10)   # Can force axes to be equal size to stop clusters being distorted in one # direction embed_plot(pca_iris$x, iris$Petal.Length,   color_scheme = "RColorBrewer::Blues",   equal_axes = TRUE )

text: coords Matrix embedded coordinates, many rows observations, 2 columns. x Either data frame column can used derive suitable vector colors. Ignored colors provided. colors Vector containing colors coordinate. color_scheme color scheme. See Details. Ignored colors specified. num_colors Number distinct colors use palette, x numeric vector, assumption palette continuous (probably ). Ignored x numeric vector. set NULL, set length(x). alpha_scale Scale opacity alpha colors, 0 1. Useful increasing transparency points, especially large plots lots overlap. limits range colors map mapping numeric vector. specified, range x. useful external absolute scale used. Ignored x numeric vector. clip_limit_values TRUE (default) limits provided, value lies outside limits clipped limiting values. tooltip = NULL original values preserved tooltip. clip_limit_values = FALSE, values outside limits shown "missing" color. title Title plot. show_legend TRUE, display categorical legend numeric colorbar suitable value provided x (one can found). cex Size points. Ignored text provided. text Vector label text display instead point, including numeric color scales. labels long data set large, unlikely legible, occasionally useful. tooltip Vector tooltip text, displayed point hovered . Without , hover text uses text, resolved labels, always retains original row-number prefix. Explicit colors alone used hover labels. equal_axes TRUE, use common coordinate range equal physical units X Y axes. pc_axes TRUE, coords replaced first two (unscaled) principal components, effect rotating data (potential reflection) main variance aligns along X-axis. scaling effect. xlim Vector two numeric values give numeric extent X-axis PCA rotation. Ignored equal_axes = TRUE. ylim Vector two numeric values give numeric extent Y-axis PCA rotation. Ignored equal_axes = TRUE. rev Logical indicating whether generated palettes continuous scales reversed. Explicit colors unchanged. verbose TRUE, log messages console, mainly searching suitable color column dataframe.

title: Embedding Plot Using the JavaScript Library Plotly — embed_plotly
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() # Embed with PCA pca_iris <- stats::prcomp(iris[, -5], retx = TRUE, rank. = 2)  # Load the plotly package library("plotly")  # Visualize the resulting embedding, colored by iris species, using the # rainbow color scheme embed_plotly(pca_iris$x, iris$Species,   color_scheme = rainbow,   title = "iris PCA" )  # topo.colors scheme embed_plotly(pca_iris$x, iris$Species, color_scheme = topo.colors)  # Pass in data frame and it will use the last (in this case, only) factor # column it finds embed_plotly(pca_iris$x, iris)  # Don't have to see a legend if the tooltips will do embed_plotly(pca_iris$x, iris, show_legend = FALSE)  # Custom tooltips embed_plotly(pca_iris$x, iris, tooltip = paste("Species:", iris$Species))  # Use the "Dark2" RColorBrewer scheme embed_plotly(pca_iris$x, iris, color_scheme = "RColorBrewer::Dark2")  # Can plot the category names instead of points, but looks bad if they're # long (or the dataset is large) embed_plotly(pca_iris$x, iris$Species, cex = 0.5, text = iris$Species)  # Visualize numeric value (petal length) as a color embed_plotly(pca_iris$x, iris$Petal.Length, color_scheme = "RColorBrewer::Blues")  # Can force axes to be equal size to stop cluster being distorted in one # direction embed_plotly(pca_iris$x, iris$Petal.Length,   color_scheme = "RColorBrewer::Blues",   equal_axes = TRUE ) }

title: Turbo Color Palette — turbo
text: Create vector n contiguous colors using Turbo rainbow colormap.

title: Turbo Color Palette — turbo
text: n number colors (>= 1) palette. start (corrected) hue [0, 1] rainbow begins. end (corrected) hue [0, 1] rainbow ends. rev logical indicating whether ordering colors reversed.

title: Turbo Color Palette — turbo
text: character vector cv containing n hex color codes. can used either create user-defined color palette subsequent graphics passing cv grDevices::palette(), col = specification graphics functions par.

title: Turbo Color Palette — turbo
text: Mikhailkov, . (2019). Google AI Blog: Turbo, Improved Rainbow Colormap Visualization. https://ai.googleblog.com/2019/08/turbo-improved-rainbow-colormap-.html. Accessed August 26, 2020.

title: Turbo Color Palette — turbo
code: # use like e.g. grDevices::rainbow pie(rep(1, 12), col = rainbow(12), main = "RGB/HSV")  pie(rep(1, 12), col = turbo(12), main = "turbo")

title: vizier 0.6.1
text: July 17 2026: many years, ggplot2 support back via embed_ggplot(). numeric color vectors now default sequential HCL Viridis palette. Categorical -input row colors use built-Polychrome 36 palette available, HCL Dynamic fallback. fix bug rev = TRUE, wrong color assigned observation. minor fixes top corner cases (e.g. ties, missing data). equal_axes = TRUE now also preserves equal physical X/Y units base Plotly output. Plotly numeric scales retain native continuous colorbar. fix regression Plotly tooltip content showed literal input colors. text labels work numeric values. July 4 2026: use Polychrome 36 palette default categorical palette. prevent default categorical palettes changing color don’t set seed right calling embed_plot.

title: vizier 0.6.0
text: July 2 2026: General housekeeping fixed palette bugs. Plotly support stopped working point probably due palette bugs, now working . However, now optional dependency, install.packages("plotly") want use .

title: vizier 0.5.4
text: November 25 2023: use Polychrome generate default color schemes categorical data.

title: vizier 0.4.2
text: September 6 2020: fix alpha_scale embed_plotly. Also, new parameter: limits embed_plotly.

title: vizier 0.4.0
text: August 26 2020: version 0.4 includes following improvements fixes: Fixed bug using recent versions paletteer choosing color scheme broken. turbo colormap (based github gist) added turbo function. New argument rev reverse ordering colors palette. useful comparing turbo rainbow palettes turbo goes blue red. new color palettes R 4.0, can pass name, e.g. color_scheme = "Okabe-Ito". colorRampPalette used need interpolate palette (.e. ask colors exist palette). Colors now returned order appear palette.

title: vizier 0.2.0
text: September 27 2018: Color schemes embed_plotly badly messed . now fixed. now also control whether interpolate discrete palette.

title: vizier 0.0.0.9000
text: August 10 2018: Last MIT-licensed version. Future versions GPL 3+.
